hero_summary: Ajam Murassa is a maqam name found in some modern Arabic guides to derived maqamat, but it is not among the heavily documented and widely theorized maqamat. It is usually presented as very close to Ajam Ushayran, with a distinctive coloring on the fourth degree.
short_identity: A relatively lightly documented Ajam-family maqam that may be understood in practice as a jeweled or colored form of Ajam Ushayran rather than a fully distant modal world of its own.

quick_facts.karar: Ajam Ashiran (B-flat below / low B-flat tonic)
quick_facts.ghammaz: Jaharkah (F) in the upper practical continuation
quick_facts.lower_jins: Jins Ajam Murassa on Ajam Ashiran
quick_facts.upper_jins: Jins Ajam in the practical upper block naming
quick_facts.character: Very close to Ajam Ushayran, with a distinctive internal coloring on the fourth degree
quick_facts.common_start: It stabilizes Ajam Ashiran below, reveals the special colored fourth, then continues upward through Ajam on Jaharkah

sayr.summary: Ajam Murassa is a maqam name that appears in some modern Arabic educational guides dealing with derived maqamat, but it is not one of the maqamat richly documented in major theoretical literature. The clearest practical description presents it as beginning with Jins Ajam Murassa on Ajam Ashiran (low B-flat tonic), followed by Jins Ajam on Jaharkah (F). The same teaching material explains that it is very close to Ajam Ushayran, but differs in the fourth degree, which is colored in a ‘murassa’ way.
resting_tones: Ajam Ashiran (low B-flat): the final tonic in the practical explanation
Jaharkah (F): the practical upper point where the Ajam continuation appears
The specially colored fourth degree: the internal marker that differentiates it from ordinary Ajam Ushayran
motion_notes: Because detailed published sayr descriptions are scarce, the safest explanation is practical and structural rather than overly prescriptive
Its character does not arise from a change of tonic, but from an internal coloring inside the lower framework of the maqam
In practice it may be understood as stabilizing Ajam Ashiran below, then bringing out the special fourth-degree color, then continuing upward through Ajam on Jaharkah
It is better understood as a ‘murassa’ form of Ajam Ushayran than as a radically separate maqamic universe
common_path: Establish the tonic on Ajam Ashiran
Reveal the colored fourth degree in the lower region
Continue upward through Ajam on Jaharkah
Return to the tonic while preserving the special lower coloring

common_modulations: It is very close to Ajam Ushayran, and the major practical distinction lies in the differently colored fourth degree
It belongs to the Ajam family and should not be described as a remote non-Ajam maqam
Its available explanations are more educational-practical than fully classical-theoretical, so it is best presented with explicit caution

reference_recordings.title: Acem Murassa Peşrev
reference_recordings.note: The existence of works under this title in catalogues shows that the name has historical repertoire presence, even if the theoretical explanation remains limited
